---
title: "Routes"
format: dashboard
---

## Snapshot

### Route Summaries

<table class="dataframe table table-striped table-sm">
  <thead>
    <tr style="text-align: left;">
      <th>Route</th>
      <th>Miles</th>
      <th>Elevation Gain (ft)</th>
      <th>Steep Climbing Miles</th>
      <th>Max Elevation (ft)</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><a href="routes/arlington.html">Arlington Gravel Loop</a></td>
      <td>14.89</td>
      <td>1761.81</td>
      <td>0.58</td>
      <td>913.71</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><a href="routes/grizzly.html">Grizzly Peak</a></td>
      <td>13.91</td>
      <td>1459.97</td>
      <td>0.32</td>
      <td>1669.95</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><a href="routes/tunnel.html">Tunnel to Pinehurst</a></td>
      <td>22.63</td>
      <td>3002.30</td>
      <td>1.92</td>
      <td>1430.12</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Notes

### Editorial Direction

- Use this page for cross-route comparison.
- Use Route Notes for maps and more route-specific data.
